name: Zerodha Trade Execution — gRPC Service
scenario: Zerodha's OrderService needs to call the TradeExecutionService at 10,000 requests/second. REST with JSON parsing overhead is creating latency spikes at peak load. Migrate to gRPC: strict contract, binary serialization, HTTP/2 multiplexing.

core.description: ZerodhaTradeService @GrpcService extending generated ImplBase. Constructor injection of TradeRepository @Service and AuditLogger @Service.
placeOrder(PlaceOrderRequest, StreamObserver<PlaceOrderResponse>): validate symbol not blank + qty > 0. INVALID_ARGUMENT on failure. Save + audit + onNext + onCompleted on success.
getOrder(GetOrderRequest, StreamObserver<PlaceOrderResponse>): NOT_FOUND if order doesn't exist. Return order on success.
LoggingServerInterceptor @GrpcGlobalServerInterceptor: log method name + call count. AtomicInteger counter per method.
TradeClientService @Service: @GrpcClient("zerodha-trade") stub field. placeOrder() wraps stub call. Catches StatusRuntimeException: DEADLINE_EXCEEDED → retry once. UNAVAILABLE → throw immediately.
@SpringBootTest (4 tests): valid order placed, symbol empty → INVALID_ARGUMENT, order not found → NOT_FOUND, deadline exceeded → retried. All pass.
application.yml: grpc.server.port=9090, grpc.client.zerodha-trade.address=static://localhost:9090 with plaintext and 5s deadline.

bonus.description: MerchantContextInterceptor @GrpcGlobalServerInterceptor: extract x-merchant-id from Metadata headers, attach to gRPC Context. Service can access via Context.key. Test: metadata value propagated correctly.
GrpcHealthIndicator @Component implements HealthIndicator: checks if gRPC server is SERVING. Spring Actuator GET /actuator/health shows grpc component. Test: Health.up() when SERVING.
TrafficDistributor @Service: round-robin across 3 pod addresses, skip unhealthy pods. Test: 9 calls → 3 hits per pod, unhealthy pod gets 0.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. onCompleted() is MANDATORY after onNext() for unary RPCs — client hangs forever if missing. onError() vs onCompleted() are mutually exclusive: call one OR the other, never both. @GrpcGlobalServerInterceptor: auto-registered with all @GrpcService beans in the application context. StatusRuntimeException: catch this specifically (not generic Exception) for gRPC errors — it contains the Status.Code. application.yml grpc.client.NAME.address: the NAME in the YAML must match the @GrpcClient("NAME") string exactly (case-sensitive).
